          Victoria Li

Tel: (647)-517-2938            Email: v42li@uwaterloo.ca         Open PDF: Victoria-Resume

Education: Candidate for Biomedical Engineering – BASc             Home: Thornhill, Ontario L4J7V3

Summary of Qualifications

  • Experienced in C# and Java by producing games using graphic user interface, receiving a grade of A+
  • Familiarity with tools such as Eclipse, Visual Studio Code and automation
  • Currently learning C++ in a data structures and algorithms course
  • Impressive leadership, communication and initiative skills in team projects by achieving top 10 internationally in the integrated marketing campaign event

Awards

  • Recipient of Waterloo President’s Scholarship
  • Student Council Award (2019) Demonstrated leadership and initiative within the school community
  • DECA (Distributive Executive Clubs of America) Internationals Top 10 (2019)
  • Gold championship volleyball medal - Ontario Volleyball Association (2015)

Projects

BUSINESS EXECUTIVE/MAYA MEMBER – UW Nanorobotics Group Current

  • Rebuilt the website to update recent projects (MAYA, Vision) and accomplishments using WordPress to maintain an online presence
  • Created and delivered presentations to funding committees Received 90% of funding requests from three sources which allowed the team to make further progress in their projects
  • Performed superconductor testing with vibrometers and collected velocity and frequency data to create a stable flux system

UNLIMBITED TEAM MEMBER – Biomechatronics Waterloo Current

  • Attended sessions on machine learning using Python for medical devices
  • Took imitative to print parts of a prosthetic hand using 3D technology

HACKER (HACKATHON) – Starterhacks Jan 2020

  • Developed a web page using HTML and CSS for volunteers and homeless shelters to connect, and used C# to create a weighting/rank system

TEAM LEAD – BME 161 Design Project Sept 2019 – Dec 2019

  • Developed and designed a medium fidelity product to solve a problem space for deaf users to better detect their surroundings
  • Prototyped the device using 3D printing technology and SolidWorks Received praise from the associate chair of Systems Design Engineering

JAVA PROJECTS – Thornhill Secondary School June 2018

  • Designed the components of the graphics and programmed the leaderboards and controlling the fish movement across the screen
  • Developed a variety of test cases to perform quality tests to ensure the game runs smoothly with no errors

PRESIDENT – HOSA (Health Occupations Students of America) Sept 2018 – June 2019

  • Organized weekly meetings and increased member engagement from previous years by 20% (30 to 37 members) by running campaigns

Work Experience

TEACHING ASSISTANT – Spirit of Math Sept 2016 – June 2019

  • Reinforced lessons presented by teachers by reviewing key material and implementing extra help sessions to improve overall grades
  • Recommended for individual one-on-one tutoring sessions

SERVER – Marigolds and Onions Jul 2015 – Aug 2016

  • Provided high quality customer service by communicating to Rogers Cup volunteer and media personnel and received praise from manager

Volunteer Work

York University Science

  • Engagement Camp Counsellor - Planned and organized activities with limited resources for youth to inspire their interest in science and was able to increase engagement for every session

Night It Up General Volunteer

  • Raised funds for the ALS double play beneficiary by providing youth with hands-on activities
